commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a read-only token export path, timeout-aware auth-list fallback behavior, and regression tests for legacy token reads and keyring timeout handling.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection at medium confidence: current main exports through the migrating token getter, and auth-list fallback can call `Keys` plus per-token `GetToken` again after list errors. I did not run a live macOS Keychain or SecretService prompt scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] External contributor proof is missing and the auth/keyring behavior is OS-dependent; no narrow code defect was found for an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(auth): Avoid keyring prompt storms
target.body:
Keychain-backed reads can currently fan out into extra prompts in read-oriented commands. `auth tokens export` uses the normal token getter, which can migrate legacy default-client tokens by writing a primary key and reading it back, and `auth list` falls back to per-token probing after a keyring timeout.

This changes export to use a read-only token path when the store supports it, keeps legacy migration for normal token reads, and stops `auth list` fallback probing once the keyring has already timed out. It also preserves the actual legacy-read error when the primary key is missing but the legacy key read fails.

The user-facing effect is that export/list paths avoid turning one blocked Keychain operation into multiple password prompts while retaining existing legacy-token compatibility.
target.author: lox
